metadata.title: Sayan Dent - Professional Dental Care
metadata.description: Professional dental care for the whole family. Implants, orthodontics, teeth whitening, and more.

hero.shortDescription: Professional dental care for the whole family. Modern equipment, experienced doctors, and a comfortable environment.

services.subtitle: Only the services we actually offer — nothing made up.

pricing.subtitle: We keep our pricing fair and transparent — no surprises.
pricing.disclaimer: * Final price may vary by case and is confirmed after consultation.

treatments.subtitle: A short selection of what we offer — see the full list and prices in the pricing section below.

scaling.title: Scaling & Polishing
scaling.description: Removes tartar, plaque, and stains for healthier gums and a cleaner mouth.
scaling.headline: A clean, healthy mouth
scaling.methodologyDesc: Ultrasonic scaling followed by polishing with professional-grade paste.

airflow.title: Airflow Cleaning
airflow.description: Deep dental cleaning using a fine jet of water and bicarbonate.
airflow.headline: Gentle but thorough deep clean
airflow.methodologyDesc: A fine pressurized stream lifts surface stains without abrasive contact.

fluoride.title: Fluoride Treatment
fluoride.description: Strengthens enamel and helps prevent cavities.
fluoride.headline: Enamel protection
fluoride.methodologyDesc: Professional fluoride gel is applied to remineralize the enamel.

fillings.title: Dental Fillings
fillings.description: Surface, medium, or deep composite fillings to restore damaged teeth.
fillings.headline: Seamless cavity restoration
fillings.methodologyDesc: Tooth-colored composite resin matched to your natural shade.

whitening.title: Teeth Whitening
whitening.headline: A brighter, more confident smile
whitening.methodologyDesc: LED-activated peroxide gel for visible results.

veneers.title: Dental Veneers
veneers.description: Thin ceramic shells placed over the front of teeth to enhance appearance.
veneers.headline: Natural-looking smile design
veneers.methodologyDesc: Ultra-thin porcelain veneers bonded to the tooth surface.

rootCanal.title: Root Canal Treatment
rootCanal.description: Modern endodontic treatment for anterior and molar teeth.
rootCanal.headline: Save your natural tooth
rootCanal.methodologyDesc: Advanced rotary endodontics with electronic apex locator.

zirconia.title: Zirconia Crown
zirconia.description: Strong, aesthetic crowns made from zirconia ceramic.
zirconia.headline: Strength meets aesthetics
zirconia.methodologyDesc: Digitally fabricated zirconia crown bonded to the prepared tooth.

emax.title: Emax Veneer
emax.headline: Premium restoration
emax.methodologyDesc: Lithium disilicate glass ceramic for ultra-thin, lifelike veneers.

experience.how: Our experienced team has performed thousands of successful procedures.
experience.benefit1: Proven track record of successful treatments
experience.benefit2: Deep expertise across all dental specialties

doctors.how: Specialists across oral care, cosmetic, and restorative dentistry.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Find answers to common questions about our dental services

items.q: Does it hurt to get a dental implant?
items.a: Modern dental implant procedures are performed under local anesthesia and are virtually painless. Most patients report less discomfort than a tooth extraction. We also offer sedation options for anxious patients.

items.q: How often should I visit the dentist?
items.a: We recommend visiting every 6 months for a routine check-up and professional cleaning. However, if you have specific dental conditions, your dentist may recommend more frequent visits.
Yes, we work with most major dental insurance providers. Our team will help you understand your coverage and maximize your benefits. We also offer flexible payment plans.

items.q: How long does teeth whitening last?
items.a: Professional teeth whitening results typically last 1-3 years depending on your diet, oral hygiene habits, and lifestyle factors like coffee or tea consumption.

items.q: What age should children first visit the dentist?
items.a: We recommend bringing your child for their first dental visit by age 1 or within 6 months of their first tooth appearing. Early visits help establish good oral health habits.

items.q: Are braces or clear aligners better?
items.a: Both options are effective. Clear aligners are nearly invisible and removable, ideal for mild to moderate cases. Traditional braces are better for complex alignment issues. We will recommend the best option during your consultation.
